items.text:
Subject: Study Group for Biology Exam

Hi everyone,

I'm organizing a study group for the upcoming BIOL 101 final exam on December 15. We'll meet in the library's Room 204 on the following Saturdays: November 10, November 17, and November 24, from 10:00 AM to 12:00 PM. Each session will cover a specific topic: cell biology, genetics, and ecology. I've created a shared folder with practice questions and notes. Please bring your textbook and any questions you have.

To cover printing costs for handouts, I'm asking everyone to contribute $5.00. Please bring cash or use the online portal to pay by November 5. If you cannot afford it, let me know privately.

We still need a volunteer to lead the genetics session. If you're interested, reply to this email by November 3. Also, please confirm your attendance for the first session by November 1 so I can reserve the room.

Best regards,
Alex Chen

questions.stem: According to the email, what must study group members do before the first session?

options.A: Purchase a textbook for the BIOL 101 final exam
options.B: Confirm their attendance for the November 10 meeting
options.C: Lead the session on genetics for the study group

questions.explanation: The email states: 'please confirm your attendance for the first session by November 1 so I can reserve the room.' Option B is correct. Option A is incorrect because members are told to bring their textbook, not purchase a new one. Option C is incorrect because leading the genetics session is optional. Option D is incorrect because the contribution is $5.00, not $10.00.

questions.stem: How much must each student pay for the handouts?

options.A: $5.00 in cash or via the online portal
options.B: $10.00 in cash or through the library
options.C: $2.00 in cash or through a friend

questions.explanation: The email states: 'To cover printing costs for handouts, I'm asking everyone to contribute $5.00. Please bring cash or use the online portal to pay by November 5.' Option A matches. Option B, C, and D are incorrect amounts or payment methods.

questions.stem: What can be inferred about the study group sessions?

options.A: They will be held every day until the exam
options.B: They are only for students who paid the fee
options.C: They will focus on three different biology topics
options.D: They require a minimum of five participants

questions.explanation: The email says each session will cover a specific topic: cell biology, genetics, and ecology. This implies three topics. Option A is incorrect because sessions are on Saturdays only. Option B is not stated; the fee is for handouts, not attendance. Option D is not mentioned.

items.text:
Notice: Vending Machine Refund Procedure

Date: October 15, 2024

Due to a technical issue, some vending machines in Anderson Hall and the Student Union may have charged incorrect amounts. If you lost money in a machine, please follow this procedure:

- Locate the machine number (found on the front panel).
- Fill out a refund request form at the Campus Services office (Room 103, Student Union) or online at the university portal.
- Include your name, student ID, the machine number, the amount lost ($5.00 maximum per request), and the date of the transaction.
- Submit the form within 14 days of the transaction date.

Refunds will be processed within 10 business days and credited to your student account. For amounts over $5.00, please contact the vending company directly at support@vendingco.com. We apologize for the inconvenience.

Contact: Campus Services, (555) 123-4567, or visit Room 103 during office hours (9:00 AM to 4:00 PM, Monday to Friday).

questions.stem: According to the notice, what must students do to request a refund?

options.A: Contact the vending company directly via email
options.B: Complete a form and include the machine number
options.C: Visit the Student Union between 9:00 AM and 4:00 PM
options.D: Provide a receipt from the vending machine purchase

questions.explanation: The notice states: 'Fill out a refund request form... Include your name, student ID, the machine number...' Option B is correct. Option A is for amounts over $5.00. Option C is the office hours, not a requirement. Option D is not mentioned.

questions.stem: The word 'credited' in the notice is closest in meaning to:
questions.explanation: 'Credited' means the refund will be added to the student's account. Option C ('Added to an account') is correct. Option A is opposite. Option B is not mentioned. Option D is incorrect because it's not a deduction.

questions.stem: What can be inferred about refunds for amounts over $5.00?

options.A: They require a different process than smaller refunds
options.B: They are processed faster than smaller refunds
options.C: They can only be requested at the Campus Services office
options.D: They are not available for vending machine issues

questions.explanation: The notice says: 'For amounts over $5.00, please contact the vending company directly.' This implies a different procedure. Option B is not stated. Option C is incorrect because the form can be submitted online. Option D is incorrect because refunds are available.

rejected.reason: answer_position: all 3 answers are "B" — must vary
